sections.text: 5 Section 7.5 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers Information specified in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container, and in the absence of such a container, on the product itself. However, lot identification and the name and address may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to Tilsiter intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard applies to Tilsiter intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to Tilsiter intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Tilsiter is a ripened firm/semi-hard cheese in conformity with the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978). The body has a near white or ivory through to light yellow or yellow colour and a firm-textured (when pressed by thumb) texture suitable for cutting, with irregularly shaped, shiny and evenly distributed gas holes. The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without a well-dried smear-developed rind, which may be coated. For Tilsiter ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 3 weeks at 10–16°C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzymes) may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Tilsiter intended for further processing need not exhibit the same degree of ripening when justified through technical and/or trade needs.

The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without a well-dried smear-developed rind, which may be coated. For Tilsiter ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 3 weeks at 10–16°C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzymes) may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Tilsiter intended for further processing need not exhibit the same degree of ripening when justified through technical and/or trade needs.

The body has a near white or ivory through to light yellow or yellow colour and a firm-textured (when pressed by thumb) texture suitable for cutting, with irregularly shaped, shiny and evenly distributed gas holes. The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without a well-dried smear-developed rind, which may be coated. For Tilsiter ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 3 weeks at 10–16°C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzymes) may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Tilsiter intended for further processing need not exhibit the same degree of ripening when justified through technical and/or trade needs.

ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS

sections.title: Raw materials
sections.text: Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Raw materials
number=3.1
level=2
Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks.

page_spans.text: Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks.

sections.title: Permitted ingredients
sections.text: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti-caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by good manufacturing practice, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.

sections.text: Only those additives classes indicated as justified in the table below may be used for the product categories specified. Anticaking agents, colours and preservatives used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives  (CXS 192-1995) in food category 01.6.2.1 (Ripened cheese, includes rind) and only certain acidity regulators and anticaking agents in Table 3 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard. (b) For the surface of sliced, cut, shredded or grated cheese, only.  X The use of additives belonging to the class is technologically justified.  – The use of additives belonging to the class is not technologically justified.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the CAC.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:
sections.retrieval_text:
LABELLING
number=7
level=1
In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:

page_spans.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name Tilsiter may be applied in accordance with section 4.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), provided that the product is in conformity with this standard. Where customary in the country of retail sale, alternative spelling may be used. The use of the name is an option that may be chosen only if the cheese complies with this standard. Where the name is not used for a cheese that complies with this standard, the naming provisions of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) apply. The designation of products in which the fat content is below or above the reference range but above the absolute minimum specified in Section 3.3 of this standard shall be accompanied by an appropriate qualification describing the modification made or the fat content (expressed as fat in dry matter or as percentage by mass whichever is acceptable in the country of retail sale), either as part of the name or in a prominent position in the same field of vision. Suitable qualifiers are the appropriate characterizing terms specified in Section 7.3 of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) or a nutritional claim in accordance with the Guidelines for Use of Nutrition and Health Claims (CXG 23-1997).2 The designation may also be used for cut, sliced, shredded or grated products made from cheese which cheese is in conformity with this standard.

sections.title: Country of origin
sections.text: The country of origin (which means the country of manufacture, not the country in which the name originated) shall be declared. When the product undergoes substantial transformation3 in a second country, the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling.

page_spans.text: The country of origin (which means the country of manufacture, not the country in which the name originated) shall be declared. When the product undergoes substantial transformation3 in a second country, the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling.

sections.title: Declaration of milk fat content
sections.text: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.

sections.title: Date marking
sections.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information, provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.

sections.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: METHODS OF SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti-caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by good manufacturing practice, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.

prefilter.reason: Lists ingredients and processing aids relevant to cheese standards.

classification.sentence: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti-caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by good manufacturing practice, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.
classification.function: List permitted substances and conditions of use
classification.pattern: Enumerative list with conditional use exception

Permitted substances: Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/or flavour producing bacteria and other harmless micro-organisms
Rennet or other safe coagulating enzymes
Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as salt substitutes
Rice, corn and potato flours and starches as anti-caking agents
components.Condition for flours and starches: Use only on surface of cut, sliced, shredded products; amounts functionally necessary; governed by GMP; consider other anti-caking agents in Section 4
components.Reference standard exception: Notwithstanding provisions in General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978)

classification.justification: Complex permission listing multiple permitted substances with specific usage conditions, a new pattern in permissions listings.
notes: Contains enumerations and conditional exceptions

sentences.original: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

classification.pattern: [Subject] shall comply with [Referenced Standards for contaminant levels and residue limits]

maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the CAC

sentences.original: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

classification.sentence: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.function: Recommendation/Requirement
classification.pattern: It is recommended that <entity> be <action> in accordance with <standards>; the <entity> should comply with <criteria> established according to <guidelines>.

components.entity_1: the product covered by the provisions of this standard
components.standards: the appropriate sections of General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004), other relevant Codex texts
components.criteria_basis: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence expresses recommended obligations with modal verbs and recommendations, linking preparation, handling, and compliance to specific standards and microbiological criteria. There is no exact match in the known templates, and the sentence contains a compound structure with multiple recommended actions tied to standards and guidance. The modality is obligation as the core function is prescribing recommended practices that imply compliance requirements.
notes: The sentence includes multiple interconnected recommendations making it complex; no exact prior template available, so a new structural pattern reflecting recommendation and compliance obligation is generated.
Use of "It is recommended" and "should" conveys a normative obligation tone rather than permission or mere suggestion.

classification.function: Specification of applicability of additional specific provisions beyond referenced documents

classification.justification: Sentence introduces an obligation by specifying additional specific provisions apply beyond cited standards, structured as a prefatory clause followed by a statement of applicability; this precise pattern of referencing other standards and then stating specific provisions applying is not represented in known templates.
notes: The sentence functions as a normative introduction to subsequent provisions but does not impose a direct action or condition itself, leading to a new structural pattern.

sentences.original: The designation of products in which the fat content is below or above the reference range but above the absolute minimum specified in Section 3.3 of this standard shall be accompanied by an appropriate qualification describing the modification made or the fat content (expressed as fat in dry matter or as percentage by mass whichever is acceptable in the country of retail sale), either as part of the name or in a prominent position in the same field of vision.

sentences.original: The country of origin (which means the country of manufacture, not the country in which the name originated) shall be declared.

classification.sentence: When the product undergoes substantial transformation3 in a second country, the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling.
classification.function: Defining country of origin based on transformation condition
classification.pattern: Conditional statement with obligation triggered by a 'when' clause

components.condition: When the product undergoes substantial transformation3 in a second country
components.obligation: the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling

classification.justification: The sentence uses a temporal condition 'when' triggering an obligation to consider the country of transformation as origin, representing an adapted template.
notes: Typical condition with consequent obligation, adapted to a specific origin labelling rule.

sentences.original: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.

classification.sentence: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.
classification.function: Prescriptive requirement with allowable alternative methods and condition
classification.pattern: Subject shall be declared in an acceptable manner with alternative formats enumerated and a condition qualifying one option

components.subject: The milk fat content
components.manner: in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale
alternative_formats: (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter
(iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label
components.condition: provided that the number of servings is stated

classification.justification: The sentence expresses an obligation to declare the milk fat content, specifying allowed alternative expression formats and adds a condition linked to one format. This specific structure with multiple alternatives and a qualifying condition does not strongly match any known template, thus classified as a new pattern. The presence of enumeration and conditional clause within an obligation sentence supports this classification.
notes: Sentence contains an obligation with enumerated alternative formats and a conditional qualifier, a structure not common in simple obligation templates.
Potential ambiguity in whether the condition applies only to the third format or all alternatives, noted but not altering classification.

sentences.original: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information, provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.

classification.sentence: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information, provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.
classification.function: Granting Conditional Permission
classification.pattern: [Exception clause], [Permission granted], [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.exception_clause: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)
components.permission_granted: the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information
components.condition: provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission conditionally, with an initial exception clause. It does not fit known templates but clearly expresses conditional permission with an exception clause and condition, thus a new pattern is created reflecting this structure.
notes: The sentence includes a legal exception phrase and a conditional restriction, making its structure more complex than standard templates.

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.
classification.function: Prescriptive requirement
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase indicating purpose + main clause prescribing required methods and reference standards with modal verb 'shall'

components.purpose: For checking the compliance with this standard
components.required_items: methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard

classification.justification: The sentence prescribes an obligation to use specified methods for compliance checking with normative modality 'shall'. The sentence structure includes an introductory purpose phrase followed by a prescriptive main clause. No known templates match closely, so a new pattern reflecting this structure is created.
notes: The sentence combines normative obligation with purpose indication in a complex sentence; no direct template found in catalog.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR TILSITER
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento e cabeçalho, sem seções ou texto estruturado para hierarquia.

pages.title: CXS 270-1968
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento no cabeçalho e duas seções principais: a emenda de 2022 e descrição detalhada da rotulagem em contêineres não comerciais. Texto está ligado diretamente a estas seções, sem indicações de continuidade entre páginas.

A página contém três seções principais numeradas: 1. SCOPE, 2. DESCRIPTION e 3. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS, esta última com duas subseções 3.1 Raw materials e 3.2 Permitted ingredients. O texto interno está corretamente associado aos seus respectivos títulos. Algumas seções continuam na próxima página, sinalizadas com continues_on_next_page=true para a seção 3 e sua subseção 3.2.

A página 4 contém a seção 3.3 Composition, que continua da página anterior, e a seção 4 FOOD ADDITIVES. Ambas as seções têm conteúdo informativo associado, sem subdivisões detectadas nesta página. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado. A seção 3.3 foi marcada como continuação da página anterior.

Página contém as seções principais 5 CONTAMINANTS, 6 HYGIENE e 7 LABELLING, com a seção 7 subdividida em cinco subseções numeradas de 7.1 a 7.5. O conteúdo textual está corretamente associado a cada título, sem indicação de continuidade entre páginas. Cabeçalho identificado como CXS 270-1968 5.
Página contém o título principal da seção 8 METHODS OF SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS com texto explicativo associado. Cabeçalho identificado como CXS 270-1968 6. Nenhuma continuação de seção detectada.
